name: Grand Marnier Nanaimo Bars
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------GRAND MARNIER LAYER----------------------------
1. In a bowl, combine 2 cups icing sugar with softened butter and Grand Marnier or orange liqueur.
Add coarsely grated orange rind.
-----------------------------CHOCOLATE TOPPING-----------------------------
1. Stir 1 tablespoon butter into melted semisweet chocolate.
2. Combine crumbs, coconut, and pecans in a bowl.
3. In a small saucepan, gently heat butter, cocoa powder, and granulated sugar until the butter melts.
Remove from heat; whisk in beaten egg. Blend into the crumb mixture.
4. Press the mixture into a greased 9-inch square cake pan.
5. Bake at 350°F for 10 minutes. Let cool on a rack.
6. For the Grand Marnier layer, spread half of the prepared icing sugar mixture over the base.
Chill in the refrigerator for 20 minutes before cutting into bars (bars can be covered and refrigerated up to 2 weeks or frozen up to 2 months. Let soften slightly before serving).
VARIATION: CLASSIC NANAIMO BARS
Make the base and chocolate topping as above. In the layer, substitute milk for Grand Marnier; add 1/2 tsp vanilla. Substitute 2 teaspoons grated lemon rind for orange zest.
